title: Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope Original Soundtrack

{{Quote|John Williams has a score of unstinting dimension, soaring string sections and thundering basses and brilliant horns, which performed by the London Symphony Orchestra for the superlative sound system, lifts you out of your seat.|[[Wikipedia:Charles Champlin|Charles Champlin]]|<ref name="LATimes">{{WebCite|url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/movies/la-et-mn-star-wars-hails-the-once-and-future-space-western-20151202-story.html|text=From the Archives: ‘Star Wars’ hails the once and future space western|author=Charles Champlin|date=1977-05-22|archivedate=20230304001856}}</ref>}}
The first score to ''[[Star Wars]]'' was composed and conducted by [[John Williams]] and orchestrated by [[Herbert Spencer]] (who orchestrated all three films of the [[original trilogy]]), [[Alexander Courage]], [[Angela Morley]], Arthur Morton and Albert Woodbury. The score was recorded by engineer [[Eric Tomlinson]] and edited by [[Ken Wannberg]]. Recorded over fourteen sessions<ref name="souvenir program">1977 ''Star Wars'' Theatrical Souvenir Program</ref> at Anvil Studios in Denham, England from [[March 5]], 8–12, 15 and 16, [[1977]]<ref name="1977 liner notes">Liner notes of the 1977 LP soundtrack release, notes written by [[Charles Lippincott]]</ref> , the score was performed by the 87-piece [[London Symphony Orchestra]].<ref name="souvenir program" />

The film premiered on [[May 25]], 1977 and by late summer a disco version of the [[Star Wars Main Title|Star Wars Theme]] performed by [[Meco Monardo|Meco]] became America's number one song. Williams received three Grammys in February 1978 and his third Academy Award (second for Best Original Score) in April.

</gallery>

==Background==
The original soundtrack for "Star Wars" was first released on LP by [[Wikipedia:20th Century Fox Records|20th Century Fox Records]] in 1977. For this release, John Williams personally selected 74 minutes from the 88 minute score, and the cues were re-ordered and cross faded together with the intent of creating a pleasant listening experience. The versions of the cues used on this release are not the film mixes made by [[Eric Tomlinson]], but rather remixes made by [[John Neal]] specifically for the album.<ref name="1977 liner notes" /> Further, we know that John Neal made these mixes using the 16-track tapes from the recording sessions.<ref name="Chris Malone document">{{WebCite|url=https://www.malonedigital.com/starwars.pdf|text=Recording the Star Wars Saga|author=Malone, Chris|date=2012-03-18|work=[https://www.malonedigital.com Malone Digital]|format=PDF|archivedate=20220619203126}}</ref><ref name="jwfan comment about john neal auction">{{WebCite|url=https://www.jwfan.com/forums/index.php?/topic/24113-some-john-williams-master-tapes-are-on-ebay-right-now|text=Some John Williams master tapes are on ebay right now....|author=Jay|date=2014-01-19|work=[https://www.jwfan.com John Williams Fan Network]|archivedate=20220619204007}}</ref>

The original soundtrack was later released on CD by [[Wikipedia:Polydor|Polydor]] in 1986. This release used the exact same masters as the original LP, so it contains the same edited program and the same mixes by John Neal. The only real difference between the original LP and the Polydor disc is the higher quality audio of CD over vinyl.

in 1997 ([[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|''RCA]] Victor''), and 2004 (''[[Wikipedia:Sony Classical Records|Sony Classical]]''), respectively, [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope Special Edition: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack|the complete score was released in chronological order for the first time]]. The two releases present exactly the same track listing. The only difference between these editions is that the RCA release includes complete notes on each track by [[Michael Matessino]] and that the Sony Classical release does not include these notes.<ref name="SWArchive-Sony Classical Presents Star Wars  Trilogy Soundtracks on CD">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/misc/news20040820.html|text=Sony Classical Presents ''Star Wars '' Trilogy Soundtracks on CD}}</ref>

The original soundtrack masters were rereleased in a different physical format one last time by Sony Classical right before they lost the rights in 2016 as part of the [[Star Wars: The Ultimate Digital Collection|"Ultimate Digital Collection"]]. This release was sourced from a new high quality scan of the OST master, so it still featured the John Neal mixes and all the original edits.<ref name="SW-Sony Classical to Release Ultimate Editions of Original Star Wars Soundtracks">{{SW|url=news/sony-classical-to-release-ultimate-editions-of-original-star-wars-soundtracks|text=Sony Classical to Release Ultimate Editions of Original ''Star Wars'' Soundtracks|nolive=1}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.masterworksmusic.com/star-wars-ultimate-vinyl-cd-digital-box-set-force-awakens-soundtrack-today|text=STAR WARS: THE ULTIMATE VINYL, CD AND DIGITAL BOX SET | THE FORCE AWAKENS SOUNDTRACK OUT TODAY|archivedate=20151231020135}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.sony.com/content/sony/en/en_us/SCA/company-news/press-releases/sony-music-entertainment/2015/star-wars-the-ultimate-editions-of-the-original-film-soundtracks.html|date=2015-09-28|text=Star Wars™ - The Ultimate Editions Of The Original Film Soundtracks|work=Sony Music Masterworks|archivedate=20230814185222}}</ref> However, the Ultimate Digital Collection was available from sites like [[Wikipedia:HDTracks|HDTracks]] in lossless up to 192 kHz/24-bit, where the Polydor CD would have been standard CD quality which is lossless 44.1 kHz/16-bit.<ref name="jwfan post about Ultimate Digital Collection">{{WebCite|url=https://www.jwfan.com/?p=8596|text=JWFan Reviews 'Star Wars: The Ultimate Soundtrack Collection'|author=Ojala, Mikko|date=2016-01-18|archivedate=20210125010049}}</ref> The Ultimate Digital Collection is no longer available for sale, and has been unavailable since [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Records|Walt Disney Records]] took over the rights.

A three-LP 40th anniversary box set was released on [[December 1]], [[2017]] by Walt Disney Records.<ref name="SW-Star Wars: A New Hope Soundtrack Vinyl Box Set Coming December 1">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-a-new-hope-soundtrack-vinyl-box-set-coming-december-1|text=''Star Wars: A New Hope'' Soundtrack Vinyl Box Set Coming December 1}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{WebCite|date=2017-11-07|text=Star Wars: A New Hope (original Motion Picture Soundtrack) 3-LP Vinyl Album Boxed Set Of Composer John Williams' Oscar®-Winning Score To Be Released On December 1|agency=PR Newswire|publication-place=Burbank, California|url=https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/star-wars-a-new-hope-original-motion-picture-soundtrack-3-lp-vinyl-album-boxed-set-of-composer-john-williams-oscar-winning-score-to-be-released-on-december-1-300550649.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200928120659/https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/star-wars-a-new-hope-original-motion-picture-soundtrack-3-lp-vinyl-album-boxed-set-of-composer-john-williams-oscar-winning-score-to-be-released-on-december-1-300550649.html|archivedate=20230102013029}}</ref> Given that this came out before the 2018 [[Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope Remastered Soundtrack|Remastered sets]], it is likely that this used the 2016 OST master scans, just like the Ultimate Digital Collection. However, since it is an LP release, it doesn't have high quality audio and can be considered a reissue of the original 1977 LP. The third LP in this release was just a bonus disc with one siding duplicating the track "The Last Battle" again (not to be confused with the cue of the same name), and with the other side just having a fancy logo etched on it.<ref name="40th anniversary lp review">{{WebCite|url=https://www.laughingplace.com/w/featured/2017/12/20/vinyl-review-star-wars-40th-anniversary-3-lp-collectors-edition-hologram-disc|text=Vinyl Review: Star Wars 40th Anniversary 3-LP Collector's Edition with Hologram Disc|author=Reif, Alex|date=2017-12-20|archivedate=20220621181016}}</ref>

[[File:StarWars1977LP.jpg|thumb|OST cover (1977 20th Century Records)|250x250px]]

Side One:
#Main Title – 5:20
## 0:00-2:07 : [1m2] Main Title
## 2:07-end : [12m2] End Titles
#Imperial Attack – 6:10
## 0:00-3:14 : [1m3] The War (microedited : missing 23 seconds)
## 3:14-end : [1m4-2m1] The Escape Hatch
#Princess Leia's Theme – 4:18
## 0:00-end : The Princess Theme
#The Desert and the Robot Auction – 2:51
## 0:00-0:52 : [1m5-2m1] Desert Song (ending cuts off early : missing 3 seconds)
## 0:52-end : [2m3 Rev.] More Little People

Side Two:

## 0:00-2:03 : [9m3-10m1] Ben's Death
## 2:03-end : [10m2] Here They Come
#The Little People Work – 4:02
## 0:00-end : [2m3 New] The Little People
#Rescue of the Princess – 4:46
## 0:00-2:05 : [8m2 Rev.] The Rescue
## 2:05-end : [9m2] The Swashbucklers
#Inner City – 4:12
## 0:00-1:34 : [7m1 New] Is it a Bird? (ending cuts off early : missing 2 seconds)
## 1:34-3:07 : [4m2] Obi-Wan Kenobi (ending cuts off early : missing 2 seconds)
## 3:07-end : [5m6 Rev.] The Inner City (3:07-end) (microedited: missing 30 seconds)
#Cantina Band – 2:44
## 0:00-end : "Cantina Band" (ending artificially faded out)

Side Three:
#The Land of the Sandpeople – 2:50
## 0:00-1:01 : [3m3 Rev.] The Sand Speeder (ending cuts off early : missing 8 seconds)
## 1:01-end : [3m4-4m1 Rev.] The Sandman Attacks (microedited : missing 20 seconds)
#Mouse Robot and Blasting Off – 4:01
## 0:00-2:01 : [7m2] The Mouse Robot
## 2:01-end : [6m1] Blasting Away (microedited : missing 15 seconds)
#The Return Home – 2:46
## 0:00-end : [4m4A] A Home Destroyed
#The Walls Converge – 4:31
## 0:00-2:58 : [8m4] The Walls Converge (ending cuts off early : missing 9 seconds)
## 2:58-end : [8m3 Rev.] The Water Snake
#The Princess Appears – 4:04
## 0:00-1:50 : [3m1 Rev.] The Princess Appears
## 1:50-end : [3m2 New] Lost R2

Side Four:
#The Last Battle – 12:05
## 0:00-1:55 : [8m2 New] More Rescue (ending cuts off early : missing 5 seconds)
## 1:55-4:25 : [9m1] Ben Creeps Around (ending cuts off early : missing 9 seconds)
## 4:25-7:38 : [11m2 Rev.] Approaching the Target (ending cuts off early : missing 11 seconds)
## 7:38-end : [12m1] The Last Battle
#The Throne Room and End Title – 5:28
## 0:00-1:50 : [12m2] End Titles (only first 1:50)
## 1:50-2:16 : [12m2X] End Titles
## 2:16-end : [12m2] End Titles (only last 3:12)

Total Time: 74:58

==Recording Information==
===Orchestra===
'''Woodwind: '''3 flutes, 2 oboes, 3 clarinets, 2 bassoons

'''Brass: '''8 horns, 4 trumpets, 3 trombones, 2 tubas

'''Percussion: '''Timpani, 3 percussion

'''Keyboards: '''Piano, Celeste

'''Strings: '''2 harps, 14 first violins, 12 second violins, 10 violas, 10 cellos, 6 double basses

'''Cantina Band: '''3 saxophones, 1 trumpet, 1 clarinet, Fender Rhodes piano, steel drum, Arp synthesizer, drum kit, and percussion
